13:30:00.040\",\n                        \"20160525 13:30:00.050\",\n                        \"20160525 13:30:00.060\",\n                    ]\n                ),\n                \"key\": [1, 2, 1, 3, 2],\n                \"value1\": [1.1, 1.2, 1.3, 1.4, 1.5],\n            },\n            columns=[\"time\", \"key\", \"value1\"],\n        )\n\n        df2 = pd.DataFrame(\n            {\n                \"time\": to_datetime(\n                    [\n                        \"20160525 13:30:00.015\",\n                        \"20160525 13:30:00.020\",\n                        \"20160525 13:30:00.025\",\n                        \"20160525 13:30:00.035\",\n                        \"20160525 13:30:00.040\",\n                        \"20160525 13:30:00.055\",\n   \"20160525 13:30:00.030\",\n                        \"20160525 13:30:00.040\",\n                        \"20160525 13:30:00.050\",\n                        \"20160525 13:30:00.060\",\n                    ]\n                ),\n                \"key\": [1, 2, 1, 3, 2],\n                \"value1\": [1.1, 1.2, 1.3, 1.4, 1.5],\n                \"value2\": [2.2, 2.1, 2.3, 2.4, 2.7],\n            },\n            columns=[\"time\", \"key\", \"value1\", \"value2\"],\n        )\n\n        tm.assert_frame_equal(result, expected)\n\n    def test_on_float(self):\n        # mimics how to determine the minimum-price variation\n        df1 = pd.DataFrame(\n            {\n                \"price\": [5.01, 0.0023, 25.13, 340.05, 30.78, 1040.90, 0.0078],\n                \"symbol\": list(\"ABCDEFG\"),\n            },\n            columns=[\"symbol\", \"price\"],\n        )\n\n        df2 = pd.DataFrame(\n            {\"price\": [0.0, 1.0, 100.0], \"mpv\": [0.0001, 0.01, 0.05]},\n            columns=[\"price\", \"mpv\"],\n        )\n\n        df1 = df1.sort_values(\"price\").reset_index(drop=True)\n\n        result = merge_asof(df1, df2, on=\"price\")\n\n        expected = pd.DataFrame(\n            {\n                \"symbol\": list(\"BGACEDF\"),\n                \"price\": [0.0023, 0.0078, 5.01, 25.13, 30.78, 340.05, 1040.90],\n                \"mpv\": [0.0001, 0.0001, 0.01, 0.01, 0.01, 0.05, 0.05],\n            },\n            columns=[\"symbol\", \"price\", \"mpv\"],\n        )\n\n        tm.assert_frame_equal(result, expected)\n\n    def test_on_specialized_type(self, any_real_numpy_dtype):\n        # see gh-13936\n        dtype = np.dtype(any_real_numpy_dtype).type\n\n        df1 = pd.DataFrame(\n            {\"value\": [5, 2, 25, 100, 78, 120, 79], \"symbol\": list(\"ABCDEFG\")},\n            columns=[\"symbol\", \"value\"],\n        )\n        df1.value = dtype(df1.value)\n\n        df2 = pd.DataFrame(\n            {\"value\": [0, 80, 120, 125], \"result\": list(\"xyzw\")},\n            columns=[\"value\", \"result\"],\n        )\n        df2.value = dtype(df2.value)\n\n        df1 = df1.sort_values(\"value\").reset_index(drop=True)\n        result = merge_asof(df1, df2, on=\"value\")\n\n        expected = pd.DataFrame(\n            {\n                \"symbol\": list(\"BACEGDF\"),\n                \"value\": [2, 5, 25, 78, 79, 100, 120],\n                \"result\": list(\"xxxxxyz\"),\n            },\n            columns=[\"symbol\", \"value\", \"result\"],\n        )\n        expected.value = dtype(expected.value)\n\n        tm.assert_frame_equal(result, expected)\n\n    def test_on_specialized_type_by_int(self, any_real_numpy_dtype):\n        # see gh-13936\n        dtype = np.dtype(any_real_numpy_dtype).type\n\n        df1 = pd.DataFrame(\n            {\n                \"value\": [5, 2, 25, 100, 78, 120, 79],\n                \"key\": [1, 2, 3, 2, 3, 1, 2],\n                \"symbol\": list(\"ABCDEFG\"),\n            },\n            columns=[\"symbol\", \"key\", \"value\"],\n        )\n        df1.value = dtype(df1.value)\n\n        df2 = pd.DataFrame(\n            {\"value\": [0, 80, 120, 125], \"key\": [1, 2, 2, 3], \"result\": list(\"xyzw\")},\n            columns=[\"value\", \"key\", \"result\"],\n        )\n        df2.value = dtype(df2.value)\n\n        df1 = df1.sort_values(\"value\").reset_index(drop=True)\n        result = merge_asof(df1, df2, on=\"value\", by=\"key\")\n\n        expected = pd.DataFrame(\n            {\n                \"symbol\": list(\"BACEGDF\"),\n                \"key\": [2, 1, 3, 3, 2, 2, 1],\n                \"value\": [2, 5, 25, 78, 79, 100, 120],\n                \"result\": [np.nan, \"x\", np.nan, np.nan, np.nan, \"y\", \"x\"],\n            },\n            columns=[\"symbol\", \"key\", \"value\", \"result\"],\n        )\n        expected.value = dtype(expected.value)\n\n        tm.assert_frame_equal(result, expected)\n\n    def test_on_float_by_int(self):\n        # type specialize both \"by\" and \"on\" parameters\n        df1 = pd.DataFrame(\n            {\n                \"symbol\": list(\"AAABBBCCC\"),\n                \"exch\": [1, 2, 3, 1, 2, 3, 1, 2, 3],\n                \"price\": [\n                    3.26,\n                    3.2599,\n                    3.2598,\n                    12.58,\n                    12.59,\n                    12.5,\n                    378.15,\n                    378.2,\n                    378.25,\n                ],\n            },\n            columns=[\"symbol\", \"exch\", \"price\"],\n        )\n\n        df2 = pd.DataFrame(\n            {\n                \"exch\": [1, 1, 1, 2, 2, 2, 3, 3, 3],\n                \"price\": [0.0, 1.0, 100.0, 0.0, 5.0, 100.0, 0.0, 5.0, 1000.0],\n                \"mpv\": [0.0001, 0.01, 0.05, 0.0001, 0.01, 0.1, 0.0001, 0.25, 1.0],\n            },\n            columns=[\"exch\", \"price\", \"mpv\"],\n        )\n\n        df1 = df1.sort_values(\"price\").reset_index(drop=True)\n        df2 = df2.sort_values(\"price\").reset_index(drop=True)\n\n        result = merge_asof(df1, df2, on=\"price\", by=\"exch\")\n\n        expected = pd.DataFrame(\n            {\n                \"symbol\": list(\"AAABBBCCC\"),\n                \"exch\": [3, 2, 1, 3, 1, 2, 1, 2, 3],\n                \"price\": [\n                    3.2598,\n                    3.2599,\n                    3.26,\n                    12.5,\n                    12.58,\n                    12.59,\n                    378.15,\n                    378.2,\n                    378.25,\n                ],\n                \"mpv\": [0.0001, 0.0001, 0.01, 0.25, 0.01, 0.01, 0.05, 0.1, 0.25],\n            },\n            columns=[\"symbol\", \"exch\", \"price\", \"mpv\"],\n        )\n\n        tm.assert_frame_equal(result, expected)\n\n    def test_merge_datatype_error_raises(self, using_infer_string):\n        if using_infer_string:\n            msg = \"incompatible merge keys\"\n        else:\n            msg = r\"Incompatible merge dtype, .*, both sides must have numeric dtype\"\n\n        left = pd.DataFrame({\"left_val\": [1, 5, 10], \"a\": [\"a\", \"b\", \"c\"]})\n        right = pd.DataFrame({\"right_val\": [1, 2, 3, 6, 7], \"a\": [1, 2, 3, 6, 7]})\n\n        with pytest.raises(MergeError, match=msg):\n            merge_asof(left, right, on=\"a\")\n\n    def test_merge_datatype_categorical_error_raises(self):\n        msg = (\n            r\"incompatible merge keys \\[0\\] .* both sides category, \"\n            \"but not equal ones\"\n        )\n\n        left = pd.DataFrame(\n            {\"left_val\": [1, 5, 10], \"a\": pd.Categorical([\"a\", \"b\", \"c\"])}\n        )\n        right = pd.DataFrame(\n            {\n                \"right_val\": [1, 2, 3, 6, 7],\n                \"a\": pd.Categorical([\"a\", \"X\", \"c\", \"X\", \"b\"]),\n            }\n        )\n\n        with pytest.raises(MergeError, match=msg):\n            merge_asof(left, right, on=\"a\")\n\n    def test_merge_groupby_multiple_column_with_categorical_column(self):\n        # GH 16454\n        df = pd.DataFrame({\"x\": [0], \"y\": [0], \"z\": pd.Categorical([0])})\n        result = merge_asof(df, df, on=\"x\", by=[\"y\", \"z\"])\n        expected = pd.DataFrame({\"x\": [0], \"y\": [0], \"z\": pd.Categorical([0])})\n        tm.assert_frame_equal(result, expected)\n\n    @pytest.mark.parametrize(\n        \"func\", [lambda x: x, lambda x: to_datetime(x)], ids=[\"numeric\", \"datetime\"]\n    )\n    @pytest.mark.parametrize(\"side\", [\"left\", \"right\"])\n    def test_merge_on_nans(self, func, side):\n        # GH 23189\n        msg = f\"Merge keys contain null values on {side} side\"\n        nulls = func([1.0, 5.0, np.nan])\n        non_nulls = func([1.0, 5.0, 10.0])\n        df_null = pd.DataFrame({\"a\": nulls, \"left_val\": [\"a\", \"b\", \"c\"]})\n        df = pd.DataFrame({\"a\": non_nulls, \"right_val\": [1, 6, 11]})\n\n        with pytest.raises(ValueError, match=msg):\n            if side == \"left\":\n                merge_asof(df_null, df, on=\"a\")\n            else:\n                merge_asof(df, df_null, on=\"a\")\n\n    def test_by_nullable(self, any_numeric_ea_dtype, using_infer_string):\n        # Note: this test passes if instead of using pd.array we use\n        #  np.array([np.nan, 1]).  Other than that, I (@jbrockmendel)\n        #  have NO IDEA what the expected behavior is.\n        # TODO(GH#32306): may be relevant to the expected behavior here.\n\n        arr = pd.array([pd.NA, 0, 1], dtype=any_numeric_ea_dtype)\n        if arr.dtype.kind in [\"i\", \"u\"]:\n            max_val = np.iinfo(arr.dtype.numpy_dtype).max\n        else:\n            max_val = np.finfo(arr.dtype.numpy_dtype).max\n        # set value s.t. (at least for integer dtypes) arr._values_for_argsort\n        #  is not an injection\n        arr[2] = max_val\n\n        left = pd.DataFrame(\n            {\n                \"by_col1\": arr,\n                \"by_col2\": [\"HELLO\", \"To\", \"You\"],\n                \"on_col\": [2, 4, 6],\n                \"value\": [\"a\", \"c\", \"e\"],\n            }\n        )\n        right = pd.DataFrame(\n            {\n                \"by_col1\": arr,\n                \"by_col2\": [\"WORLD\", \"Wide\", \"Web\"],\n                \"on_col\": [1, 2, 6],\n                \"value\": [\"b\", \"d\", \"f\"],\n            }\n        )\n\n        result = merge_asof(left, right, by=[\"by_col1\", \"by_col2\"], on=\"on_col\")\n        expected = pd.DataFrame(\n            {\n                \"by_col1\": arr,\n                \"by_col2\": [\"HELLO\", \"To\", \"You\"],\n                \"on_col\": [2, 4, 6],\n                \"value_x\": [\"a\", \"c\", \"e\"],\n            }\n        )\n        expected[\"value_y\"] = np.array([np.nan, np.nan, np.nan], dtype=object)\n        if using_infer_string:\n            expected[\"value_y\"] = expected[\"value_y\"].astype(\"string[pyarrow_numpy]\")\n        tm.assert_frame_equal(result, expected)\n\n    def test_merge_by_col_tz_aware(self):\n        # GH 27642\n        if unit == \"s\":\n            pytest.skip(\n                \"This test is invalid with unit='s' because that would \"\n                \"round left['time']\"\n            )\n\n        left = pd.DataFrame(\n            list(zip([0, 5, 10, 15, 20, 25], [0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5])),\n            columns=[\"time\", \"left\"],\n        )\n\n        left[\"time\"] = pd.to_timedelta(left[\"time\"], \"ms\").astype(f\"m8[{unit}]\")\n\n        right = pd.DataFrame(\n            list(zip([0, 3, 9, 12, 15, 18], [0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5])),\n            columns=[\"time\", \"right\"],\n        )\n\n        right[\"time\"] = pd.to_timedelta(right[\"time\"], \"ms\").astype(f\"m8[{unit}]\")\n\n        expected = pd.DataFrame(\n            list(\n                zip(\n                    [0, 5, 10, 15, 20, 25],\n                    [0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5],\n                    [0, np.nan, 2, 4, np.nan, np.nan],\n                )\n            ),\n            columns=[\"time\", \"left\", \"right\"],\n        )\n\n        expected[\"time\"] = pd.to_timedelta(expected[\"time\"], \"ms\").astype(f\"m8[{unit}]\")\n\n        result = merge_asof(\n            left, right, on=\"time\", tolerance=Timedelta(\"1ms\"), direction=\"nearest\"\n        )\n\n        tm.assert_frame_equal(result, expected)\n\n    def test_int_type_tolerance(self, any_int_dtype):\n        # [1, 6, np.nan]}\n        )\n\n        result = merge_asof(left, right, on=\"a\", direction=\"forward\")\n        tm.assert_frame_equal(result, expected)\n\n    def test_examples4(self):\n        \"\"\"doc-string examples\"\"\"\n        # GH14887\n\n        left = pd.DataFrame({\"a\": [1, 5, 10], \"left_val\": [\"a\", \"b\", \"c\"]})\n        right = pd.DataFrame({\"a\": [1, 2, 3, 6, 7], = merge_asof(trades, quotes, on=\"time\", by=\"ticker\")\n        tm.assert_frame_equal(result, expected)\n\n    def test_basic_categorical(self, trades, asof, quotes):\n        expected = asof\n        trades.ticker = trades.ticker.astype(\"category\")\n        quotes.ticker = quotes.ticker.astype(\"category\")\n        expected.ticker = expected.ticker.astype(\"category\")\n\n        result = merge_asof(trades, quotes, on=\"time\", by=\"ticker\")\n        tm.assert_frame_equal(result, expected)\n\n    def test_basic_left_index(self, trades, asof, quotes):\n        # GH14253\n        expected = asof\n        trades = trades.set_index(\"time\")\n\n        result = merge_asof(\n            trades, quotes, left_index=True, right_on=\"time\", by=\"ticker\"\n        )\n        # left-only index uses right\"s index, oddly\n        expected.index = result.index\n        # time column appears after left\"s columns\n        expected = expected[result.columns]\n        tm.assert_frame_equal(result, expected)\n\n    def test_basic_right_index(self, trades, asof, quotes):\n        expected = asof\n        quotes = quotes.set_index(\"time\")\n\n        result = merge_asof(\n            trades, quotes, left_on=\"time\", right_index=True, by=\"ticker\"\n        )\n        tm.assert_frame_equal(result, expected)\n\n    def test_basic_left_index_right_index(self, trades, asof, quotes):\n        expected = asof.set_index(\"time\")\n        trades = trades.set_index(\"time\")\n        quotes = quotes.set_index(\"time\")\n\n        result = merge_asof(\n            trades, quotes, left_index=True, right_index=True, by=\"ticker\"\n        )\n        tm.assert_frame_equal(result, expected)\n\n    def test_multi_index_left(self, trades, quotes):\n        # MultiIndex is prohibited\n        trades = trades.set_index([\"time\", \"price\"])\n        quotes = quotes.set_index(\"time\")\n        with pytest.raises(MergeError, match=\"left can only have one index\"):\n            merge_asof(trades, quotes, left_index=True, right_index=True)\n\n    def test_multi_index_right(self, trades, quotes):\n        # MultiIndex is prohibited\n        trades = trades.set_index(\"time\")\n        quotes = quotes.set_index([\"time\", \"bid\"])\n        with pytest.raises(MergeError, match=\"right can only have one index\"):\n            merge_asof(trades, quotes, left_index=True, right_index=True)\n\n    def test_on_and_index_left_on(self, trades, quotes):\n        # \"on\" parameter and index together is prohibited\n        trades = trades.set_index(\"time\")\n        quotes = quotes.set_index(\"time\")\n        msg = 'Can only pass argument \"left_on\" OR \"left_index\" not both.'\n        with pytest.raises(MergeError, match=msg):\n            merge_asof(\n                trades, quotes, left_on=\"price\", left_index=True, right_index=True\n            )\n\n    def test_on_and_index_right_on(self, trades, quotes):\n        trades = trades.set_index(\"time\")\n        quotes = quotes.set_index(\"time\")\n        msg = 'Can only pass argument \"right_on\" OR \"right_index\" not both.'\n        with pytest.raises(MergeError, match=msg):\n            merge_asof(\n                trades, quotes, right_on=\"bid\", left_index=True, right_index=True\n            )\n\n    def test_basic_left_by_right_by(self, trades, asof, quotes):\n        # GH14253\n        expected = asof\n\n        result = merge_asof(\n            trades, quotes, on=\"time\", tm.assert_frame_equal(result, expected)\n\n    def test_multiby(self):\n        # GH13936\n        trades = pd.DataFrame(\n            {\n                \"time\": to_datetime(\n           [\n                [to_datetime(\"20160602\"), 1, \"a\"],\n                [to_datetime(\"20160602\"), 2, \"a\"],\n                [to_datetime(\"20160603\"), 1, \"b\"],\n                [to_datetime(\"20160603\"), 2, \"b\"],\n            ],\n            columns=[\"time\", \"k1\", \"k2\"],\n        ).set_index(\"time\")\n        # different dtype for the index\n        left.index = left.index - pd.Timestamp(0)\n\n        right = pd.DataFrame(\n            [\n                [to_datetime(\"20160502\"), 1, \"a\", 1.0],\n                [to_datetime(\"20160502\"), 2, \"a\", 2.0],\n                [to_datetime(\"20160503\"), 1, \"b\", 3.0],\n                [to_datetime(\"20160503\"), 2, \"b\", 4.0],\n            ],\n            columns=[\"time\", \"k1\", \"k2\", \"value\"],\n        ).set_index(\"time\")\n\n        msg = \"incompatible merge keys\"\n        with pytest.raises(MergeError, match=msg):\n            merge_asof(left, right, left_index=True, right_index=True, by=[\"k1\", \"k2\"])\n\n    def test_multiby_indexed(self):\n        # GH15676\n        left = pd.DataFrame(\n            [\n                [to_datetime(\"20160602\"), 1, \"a\"],\n                [to_datetime(\"20160602\"), 2, \"a\"],\n                [to_datetime(\"20160603\"), 1, \"b\"],\n                [to_datetime(\"20160603\"), 2, \"b\"],\n            ],\n            columns=[\"time\", \"k1\", \"k2\"],\n        ).set_index(\"time\")\n\n        right = pd.DataFrame(\n            [\n                [to_datetime(\"20160502\"), 1, \"a\", 1.0],\n                [to_datetime(\"20160502\"), 2, \"a\", 2.0],\n                [to_datetime(\"20160503\"), 1, \"b\", 3.0],\n                [to_datetime(\"20160503\"), 2, \"b\", 4.0],\n            ],\n            columns=[\"time\", \"k1\", \"k2\", \"value\"],\n        ).set_index(\"time\")\n\n        expected = pd.DataFrame(\n            [\n                [to_datetime(\"20160602\"), 1, \"a\", 1.0],\n                [to_datetime(\"20160602\"), 2, \"a\", 2.0],\n                [to_datetime(\"20160603\"), 1, \"b\", 3.0],\n                [to_datetime(\"20160603\"), 2, \"b\", 4.0],\n            ],\n            columns=[\"time\", \"k1\", \"k2\", \"value\"],\n        ).set_index(\"time\")\n\n        result = merge_asof(\n            left, right, left_index=True, right_index=True, by=[\"k1\", \"k2\"]\n        )\n\n        tm.assert_frame_equal(expected, result)\n\n        with pytest.raises(\n            MergeError, match=\"left_by and right_by must be the same length\"\n        ):\n            merge_asof(\n                left,\n                right,\n                left_index=True,\n                right_index=True,\n                left_by=[\"k1\", \"k2\"],\n                right_by=[\"k1\"],\n            )\n\n    def test_basic2(self, datapath):\n        expected = pd.DataFrame(\n            [\n                [\n                    \"20160525 13:30:00.023\",\n tm.assert_frame_equal(result, expected)\n\n    def test_basic_no_by(self, trades, asof, quotes):\n        f = (\n            lambda x: x[x.ticker == \"MSFT\"]\n            .drop(\"ticker\", axis=1)\n            .reset_index(drop=True)\n        )\n\n        # just use a single ticker\n        expected = f(asof)\n        trades = f(trades)\n        quotes = f(quotes)\n\n        result = merge_asof(trades, quotes, on=\"time\")\n        tm.assert_frame_equal(result, expected)\n\n    def test_valid_join_keys(self, trades, quotes):\n        msg = r\"incompatible merge keys \\[1\\] .* must be the same type\"\n\n        with pytest.raises(MergeError, match=msg):\n            merge_asof(trades, quotes, left_on=\"time\", right_on=\"bid\", by=\"ticker\")\n\n        with pytest.raises(MergeError, match=\"can only asof on a key for left\"):\n            merge_asof(trades, quotes, on=[\"time\", \"ticker\"], by=\"ticker\")\n\n        with pytest.raises(MergeError, match=\"can only asof on a key for left\"):\n            merge_asof(trades, quotes, by=\"ticker\")\n\n    def test_with_duplicates(self, datapath, trades, quotes, asof):\n        q = (\n            pd.concat([quotes, quotes])\n            .sort_values([\"time\", \"ticker\"])\n            .reset_index(drop=True)\n        )\n        result = merge_asof(trades, q, on=\"time\", by=\"ticker\")\n        expected = self.prep_data(asof)\n        tm.assert_frame_equal(result, expected)\n\n    def test_with_duplicates_no_on(self):\n        df1 = pd.DataFrame({\"key\": [1, 1, 3], \"left_val\": [1, 2, 3]})\n        df2 = pd.DataFrame({\"key\": [1, 2, 2], \"right_val\": [1, 2, 3]})\n        result = merge_asof(df1, df2, on=\"key\")\n        expected = pd.DataFrame(\n            {\"key\": [1, 1, 3], \"left_val\": [1, 2, 3], \"right_val\": [1, 1, 3]}\n        )\n        tm.assert_frame_equal(result, expected)\n\n    def test_valid_allow_exact_matches(self, trades, quotes):\n        msg = \"allow_exact_matches must be boolean, passed foo\"\n\n        with pytest.raises(MergeError, match=msg):\n            merge_asof(\n                trades, quotes, on=\"time\", by=\"ticker\", allow_exact_matches=\"foo\"\n            )\n\n    def test_valid_tolerance(self, trades, quotes):\n        # dti\n        merge_asof(trades, quotes, on=\"time\", by=\"ticker\", tolerance=Timedelta(\"1s\"))\n\n        # integer\n        merge_asof(\n            trades.reset_index(),\n            quotes.reset_index(),\n            on=\"index\",\n            by=\"ticker\",\n            tolerance=1,\n        )\n\n        msg = r\"incompatible tolerance .*, must be compat with type .*\"\n\n        # incompat\n        with pytest.raises(MergeError, match=msg):\n            merge_asof(trades, quotes, on=\"time\", by=\"ticker\", tolerance=1)\n\n        # invalid\n        with pytest.raises(MergeError, match=msg):\n            merge_asof(\n                trades.reset_index(),\n                quotes.reset_index(),\n                on=\"index\",\n                by=\"ticker\",\n                tolerance=1.0,\n            )\n\n        msg = \"tolerance must be positive\"\n\n        # invalid negative\n        with pytest.raises(MergeError, match=msg):\n            merge_asof(\n                trades, quotes, on=\"time\", by=\"ticker\", tolerance=-Timedelta(\"1s\")\n            )\n\n        with pytest.raises(MergeError, match=msg):\n            merge_asof(\n                trades.reset_index(),\n                quotes.reset_index(),\n                on=\"index\",\n                by=\"ticker\",\n                tolerance=-1,\n            )\n\n    def test_non_sorted(self, trades, quotes):\n        trades = trades.sort_values(\"time\", ascending=False)\n        quotes = quotes.sort_values(\"time\", ascending=False)\n\n        # we require that we are already sorted on time & quotes\n        assert not trades.time.is_monotonic_increasing\n        assert not quotes.time.is_monotonic_increasing\n        with pytest.raises(ValueError, match=\"left keys must be sorted\"):\n            merge_asof(trades, quotes, on=\"time\", by=\"ticker\")\n\n        trades = trades.sort_values(\"time\")\n        assert trades.time.is_monotonic_increasing\n        assert not quotes.time.is_monotonic_increasing\n        with pytest.raises(ValueError, match=\"right keys must be sorted\"):\n            merge_asof(trades, quotes, on=\"time\", by=\"ticker\")\n\n        quotes = quotes.sort_values(\"time\")\n        assert trades.time.is_monotonic_increasing\n        assert quotes.time.is_monotonic_increasing\n\n        # ok, though has dupes\n        merge_asof(trades, quotes, on=\"time\", by=\"ticker\")\n\n    @pytest.mark.parametrize(\n        \"tolerance_ts\",\n        [Timedelta(\"1day\"), datetime.timedelta(days=1)],\n        ids=[\"Timedelta\", \"datetime.timedelta\"],\n    )\n    def test_tolerance(self, tolerance_ts, trades, quotes, tolerance):\n        result = merge_asof(\n            trades, quotes, on=\"time\", by=\"ticker\", tolerance=tolerance_ts\n        )\n        expected = tolerance\n        tm.assert_frame_equal(result, expected)\n\n    def test_tolerance_forward(self):\n        # GH14887\n\n        left = pd.DataFrame({\"a\": [1, 5, 10], \"left_val\": [\"a\", \"b\", \"c\"]})\n        right = pd.DataFrame({\"a\": [1, 2, 3, 7, 11], \"right_val\": [1, 2, 3, 7, 11]})\n\n        expected = pd.DataFrame(\n            {\"a\": [1, 5, 10], \"left_val\": [\"a\", \"b\", \"c\"], \"right_val\": [1, np.nan, 11]}\n        )\n\n        result = merge_asof(left, right, on=\"a\", direction=\"forward\", tolerance=1)\n        tm.assert_frame_equal(result, expected)\n\n    def test_tolerance_nearest(self):\n        # GH14887\n\n        left = pd.DataFrame({\"a\": [1, 5, 10], \"left_val\": [\"a\", \"b\", \"c\"]})\n        right = pd.DataFrame({\"a\": [1, 2, 3, 7, 11], \"right_val\": [1, 2, 3, 7, 11]})\n\n        expected = pd.DataFrame(\n            {\"a\": [1, 5, 10], \"left_val\": [\"a\", \"b\", \"c\"], \"right_val\": [1, np.nan, 11]}\n        )\n\n        result = merge_asof(left, right, on=\"a\", direction=\"nearest\", tolerance=1)\n        tm.assert_frame_equal(result, expected)\n\n    def test_tolerance_tz(self, unit):\n        # GH 14844\n             tz=pytz.timezone(\"UTC\"),\n                    unit=unit,\n                ),\n                \"value2\": list(\"ABCDE\"),\n            }\n        )\n        result = merge_asof(left, ),\n                \"value1\": np.arange(5),\n                \"value2\": list(\"BCDEE\"),\n            }\n        )\n        tm.assert_frame_equal(result, expected)\n\n    def test_tolerance_float(self):\n        # GH22981\n        left = pd.DataFrame({\"a\": [1.1, 3.5, 10.9], \"left_val\": [\"a\", \"b\", \"c\"]})\n        right = pd.DataFrame(\n            {\"a\": [1.0, }\n        )\n        tm.assert_frame_equal(result, expected)\n\n        result = merge_asof(\n            df1,\n            df2,\n            on=\"time\",\n            allow_exact_matches=False,\n 13:30:00.040\",\n                        \"20160525 13:30:00.050\",\n                        \"20160525 13:30:00.060\",\n                    ]\n                ),\n                \"key\": [1, 2, 1, 3, 2],\n                \"value1\": [1.1, 1.2, 1.3, 1.4, 1.5],\n            },\n            columns=[\"time\", \"key\", \"value1\"],\n        )\n\n        df2 = pd.DataFrame(\n            {\n 13:30:00.025\",\n                        \"20160525 13:30:00.035\",\n                        \"20160525 13:30:00.040\",\n                        \"20160525 13:30:00.055\",\n      ]\n                ),\n                \"key\": [1, 2, 1, 3, 2],\n                \"value1\": [1.1, 1.2, 1.3, 1.4, 1.5],\n                \"value2\": [2.2, 2.1, 2.3, 2.4, 2.7],\n            },\n minimum-price variation\n        df1 = pd.DataFrame(\n            {\n                \"price\": [5.01, 0.0023, 25.13, 340.05, 30.78, 1040.90, 0.0078],\n                \"symbol\": list(\"ABCDEFG\"),\n            },\n            columns=[\"symbol\", \"price\"],\n        )\n\n        df2 = pd.DataFrame(\n            {\"price\": [0.0, 1.0, 100.0], \"mpv\": [0.0001, 0.01, 0.05]},\n            columns=[\"price\", \"mpv\"],\n        )\n\n        df1 = df1.sort_values(\"price\").reset_index(drop=True)\n\n        result = merge_asof(df1, df2, on=\"price\")\n\n        expected = pd.DataFrame(\n            {\n                \"symbol\": list(\"BGACEDF\"),\n                \"price\": [0.0023, 0.0078, 5.01, 25.13, 30.78, 340.05, 1040.90],\n                \"mpv\": [0.0001, 0.0001, 0.01, 0.01, 0.01, 0.05, 0.05],\n            },\n            columns=[\"symbol\", \"price\", \"mpv\"],\n        )\n\n        tm.assert_frame_equal(result, expected)\n\n    def test_on_specialized_type(self, any_real_numpy_dtype):\n        # see gh-13936\n        dtype = np.dtype(any_real_numpy_dtype).type\n\n        df1 = pd.DataFrame(\n            {\"value\": [5, 2, 25, 100, 78, 120, 79], \"symbol\": list(\"ABCDEFG\")},\n            columns=[\"symbol\", \"value\"],\n        )\n        df1.value = dtype(df1.value)\n\n        df2 = pd.DataFrame(\n            {\"value\": [0, 80, 120, 125], \"result\": list(\"xyzw\")},\n            columns=[\"value\", \"result\"],\n        )\n        df2.value = dtype(df2.value)\n\n        df1 = df1.sort_values(\"value\").reset_index(drop=True)\n        result = merge_asof(df1, df2, on=\"value\")\n\n        expected = pd.DataFrame(\n            {\n                \"symbol\": list(\"BACEGDF\"),\n                \"value\": [2, 5, 25, 78, 79, 100, 120],\n                \"result\": list(\"xxxxxyz\"),\n            },\n            columns=[\"symbol\", \"value\", \"result\"],\n        )\n        expected.value = dtype(expected.value)\n\n        tm.assert_frame_equal(result, expected)\n\n    def test_on_specialized_type_by_int(self, any_real_numpy_dtype):\n        # see gh-13936\n        dtype = np.dtype(any_real_numpy_dtype).type\n\n        df1 = pd.DataFrame(\n            {\n                \"value\": [5, 2, 25, 100, 78, 120, 79],\n                \"key\": [1, 2, 3, 2, 3, 1, 2],\n                \"symbol\": list(\"ABCDEFG\"),\n            },\n            columns=[\"symbol\", \"key\", \"value\"],\n        )\n        df1.value = dtype(df1.value)\n\n        df2 = pd.DataFrame(\n            {\"value\": [0, 80, 120, 125], \"key\": [1, 2, 2, 3], \"result\": list(\"xyzw\")},\n            columns=[\"value\", \"key\", \"result\"],\n        )\n        df2.value = dtype(df2.value)\n\n        df1 = df1.sort_values(\"value\").reset_index(drop=True)\n        result = merge_asof(df1, df2, on=\"value\", by=\"key\")\n\n        expected = pd.DataFrame(\n            {\n                \"symbol\": list(\"BACEGDF\"),\n                \"key\": [2, 1, 3, 3, 2, 2, 1],\n                \"value\": [2, 5, 25, 78, 79, 100, 120],\n                \"result\": [np.nan, \"x\", np.nan, np.nan, np.nan, \"y\", \"x\"],\n            },\n            columns=[\"symbol\", \"key\", \"value\", \"result\"],\n        )\n        expected.value = dtype(expected.value)\n\n        tm.assert_frame_equal(result, expected)\n\n    def test_on_float_by_int(self):\n        # type specialize both \"by\" and \"on\" parameters\n        df1 = pd.DataFrame(\n            {\n                \"symbol\": list(\"AAABBBCCC\"),\n                \"exch\": [1, 2, 3, 1, 2, 3, 1, 2, 3],\n                \"price\": [\n                    3.26,\n                    3.2599,\n                    3.2598,\n                    12.58,\n                    12.59,\n                    12.5,\n                    378.15,\n                    378.2,\n                    378.25,\n                ],\n            },\n            columns=[\"symbol\", \"exch\", \"price\"],\n        )\n\n        df2 = pd.DataFrame(\n            {\n                \"exch\": [1, 1, 1, 2, 2, 2, 3, 3, 3],\n                \"price\": [0.0, 1.0, 100.0, 0.0, 5.0, 100.0, 0.0, 5.0, 1000.0],\n                \"mpv\": [0.0001, 0.01, 0.05, 0.0001, 0.01, 0.1, 0.0001, 0.25, 1.0],\n            },\n            columns=[\"exch\", \"price\", \"mpv\"],\n        )\n\n        df1 = df1.sort_values(\"price\").reset_index(drop=True)\n        df2 = df2.sort_values(\"price\").reset_index(drop=True)\n\n        result = merge_asof(df1, df2, on=\"price\", by=\"exch\")\n\n        expected = pd.DataFrame(\n            {\n                \"symbol\": list(\"AAABBBCCC\"),\n                \"exch\": [3, 2, 1, 3, 1, 2, 1, 2, 3],\n                \"price\": [\n                    3.2598,\n                    3.2599,\n                    3.26,\n                    12.5,\n                    12.58,\n                    12.59,\n                    378.15,\n                    378.2,\n                    378.25,\n                ],\n                \"mpv\": [0.0001, 0.0001, 0.01, 0.25, 0.01, 0.01, 0.05, 0.1, 0.25],\n            },\n            columns=[\"symbol\", \"exch\", \"price\", \"mpv\"],\n        )\n\n        tm.assert_frame_equal(result, expected)\n\n    def test_merge_datatype_error_raises(self, using_infer_string):\n        if using_infer_string:\n            msg = \"incompatible merge keys\"\n        else:\n            msg = r\"Incompatible merge dtype, .*, both sides must have numeric dtype\"\n\n        left = pd.DataFrame({\"left_val\": [1, 5, 10], \"a\": [\"a\", \"b\", \"c\"]})\n        right = pd.DataFrame({\"right_val\": [1, 2, 3, 6, 7], \"a\": [1, 2, 3, 6, 7]})\n\n        with pytest.raises(MergeError, match=msg):\n            merge_asof(left, right, on=\"a\")\n\n    def test_merge_datatype_categorical_error_raises(self):\n        msg = (\n            r\"incompatible merge keys \\[0\\] .* both sides category, \"\n            \"but not equal ones\"\n        )\n\n        left = pd.DataFrame(\n            {\"left_val\": [1, 5, 10], \"a\": pd.Categorical([\"a\", \"b\", \"c\"])}\n        )\n pd.Categorical([0])})\n        tm.assert_frame_equal(result, expected)\n\n    @pytest.mark.parametrize(\n        \"func\", [lambda x: x, lambda x: to_datetime(x)], ids=[\"numeric\", \"datetime\"]\n    )\n    @pytest.mark.parametrize(\"side\", [\"left\", \"right\"])\n    def test_merge_on_nans(self, func, side):\n        # GH 23189\n        msg = f\"Merge keys contain null values on {side} side\"\n        nulls = func([1.0, 5.0, np.nan])\n        non_nulls = func([1.0, 5.0, 10.0])\n        df_null = pd.DataFrame({\"a\": nulls, \"left_val\": [\"a\", \"b\", \"c\"]})\n        df = pd.DataFrame({\"a\": non_nulls, \"right_val\": [1, 6, 11]})\n\n        with pytest.raises(ValueError, match=msg):\n            if side == \"left\":\n                merge_asof(df_null, df, on=\"a\")\n            else:\n                merge_asof(df, df_null, on=\"a\")\n\n    def test_by_nullable(self, any_numeric_ea_dtype, using_infer_string):\n        # Note: this test passes if instead of using pd.array we use\n        #  np.array([np.nan, 1]).  Other than that, I (@jbrockmendel)\n        #  have NO IDEA what the expected behavior is.\n        # TODO(GH#32306): may be relevant to the expected behavior here.\n\n        arr = pd.array([pd.NA, 0, 1], dtype=any_numeric_ea_dtype)\n        if arr.dtype.kind in [\"i\", \"u\"]:\n            max_val = np.iinfo(arr.dtype.numpy_dtype).max\n        else:\n            max_val = np.finfo(arr.dtype.numpy_dtype).max\n        # set value s.t. (at least for integer dtypes) arr._values_for_argsort\n        #  is not an injection\n        arr[2] = max_val\n\n        left = pd.DataFrame(\n            {\n                \"by_col1\": arr,\n                \"by_col2\": [\"HELLO\", \"To\", \"You\"],\n                \"on_col\": [2, 4, 6],\n                \"value\": [\"a\", \"c\", \"e\"],\n            }\n        )\n        right = pd.DataFrame(\n            {\n                \"by_col1\": arr,\n                \"by_col2\": [\"WORLD\", \"Wide\", \"Web\"],\n                \"on_col\": [1, 2, 6],\n                \"value\": [\"b\", \"d\", \"f\"],\n            }\n        )\n\n        result = merge_asof(left, right, by=[\"by_col1\", \"by_col2\"], on=\"on_col\")\n        expected = pd.DataFrame(\n            {\n                \"by_col1\": arr,\n                \"by_col2\": [\"HELLO\", \"To\", \"You\"],\n                \"on_col\": [2, 4, 6],\n                \"value_x\": [\"a\", \"c\", \"e\"],\n            }\n        )\n        expected[\"value_y\"] = np.array([np.nan, np.nan, np.nan], dtype=object)\n        if using_infer_string:\n            expected[\"value_y\"] = expected[\"value_y\"].astype(\"string[pyarrow_numpy]\")\n        tm.assert_frame_equal(result, expected)\n\n    def test_merge_by_col_tz_aware(self):\n        # GH 27642\n        if unit == \"s\":\n            pytest.skip(\n                \"This test is invalid with unit='s' because that would \"\n                \"round left['time']\"\n            )\n\n        left = pd.DataFrame(\n            list(zip([0, 5, 10, 15, 20, 25], [0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5])),\n            columns=[\"time\", \"left\"],\n        )\n\n        left[\"time\"] = pd.to_timedelta(left[\"time\"], \"ms\").astype(f\"m8[{unit}]\")\n\n        right = pd.DataFrame(\n            list(zip([0, 3, 9, 12, 15, 18], [0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5])),\n            columns=[\"time\", \"right\"],\n        )\n\n        right[\"time\"] = pd.to_timedelta(right[\"time\"], \"ms\").astype(f\"m8[{unit}]\")\n\n        expected = pd.DataFrame(\n            list(\n                zip(\n                    [0, 5, 10, 15, 20, 25],\n                    [0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5],\n                    [0, np.nan, 2, 4, np.nan, np.nan],\n                )\n            ),\n            columns=[\"time\", \"left\", \"right\"],\n        )\n\n        expected[\"time\"] = pd.to_timedelta(expected[\"time\"], \"ms\").astype(f\"m8[{unit}]\")\n\n        result = merge_asof(\n            left, right, on=\"time\", tolerance=Timedelta(\"1ms\"), direction=\"nearest\"\n        )\n\n        tm.assert_frame_equal(result, expected)\n\n    def test_int_type_tolerance(self, any_int_dtype):\n        # = merge_asof(trades, quotes, on=\"time\", by=\"ticker\")\n        tm.assert_frame_equal(result, expected)\n\n    def test_basic_categorical(self, trades, asof, quotes):\n        expected = asof\n        trades.ticker = trades.ticker.astype(\"category\")\n        quotes.ticker = quotes.ticker.astype(\"category\")\n        expected.ticker = expected.ticker.astype(\"category\")\n\n        result = expected = asof\n        trades = trades.set_index(\"time\")\n\n        result = merge_asof(\n            trades, quotes, left_index=True, right_on=\"time\", by=\"ticker\"\n        )\n        # left-only index uses right\"s index, oddly\n        expected.index = result.index\n        # time column appears after left\"s columns\n        expected = expected[result.columns]\n        tm.assert_frame_equal(result, expected)\n\n    def test_basic_right_index(self, trades, asof, quotes):\n        expected = asof\n        quotes = quotes.set_index(\"time\")\n\n        result = test_basic_left_index_right_index(self, trades, asof, quotes):\n        expected = asof.set_index(\"time\")\n        trades = trades.set_index(\"time\")\n        quotes = quotes.set_index(\"time\")\n\n        result = merge_asof(\n            trades, quotes, left_index=True, right_index=True, by=\"ticker\"\n        )\n        tm.assert_frame_equal(result, expected)\n\n    def test_multi_index_left(self, trades, quotes):\n        # MultiIndex is prohibited\n        trades = trades.set_index([\"time\", \"price\"])\n        quotes = quotes.set_index(\"time\")\n        with pytest.raises(MergeError, match=\"left can only have one index\"):\n            merge_asof(trades, quotes, left_index=True, right_index=True)\n\n    def test_multi_index_right(self, trades, quotes):\n        # MultiIndex is prohibited\n        trades = trades.set_index(\"time\")\n        quotes = quotes.set_index([\"time\", \"bid\"])\n        with pytest.raises(MergeError, match=\"right can only have one index\"):\n            merge_asof(trades, quotes, left_index=True, right_index=True)\n\n    def test_on_and_index_left_on(self, trades, quotes):\n        # \"on\" parameter and index together is prohibited\n        trades = trades.set_index(\"time\")\n        quotes = quotes.set_index(\"time\")\n        msg = 'Can only pass argument \"left_on\" OR \"left_index\" not both.'\n        with pytest.raises(MergeError, match=msg):\n            merge_asof(\n                trades, quotes, left_on=\"price\", left_index=True, right_index=True\n            )\n\n    def test_on_and_index_right_on(self, trades, quotes):\n        trades = trades.set_index(\"time\")\n        quotes = quotes.set_index(\"time\")\n        msg = 'Can only pass argument \"right_on\" OR \"right_index\" not both.'\n        with pytest.raises(MergeError, match=msg):\n            merge_asof(\n                trades, quotes, right_on=\"bid\", left_index=True, right_index=True\n            )\n\n    def test_basic_left_by_right_by(self, trades, asof, quotes):\n        # GH14253\n        expected = asof\n\n        result = merge_asof(\n            trades, quotes, on=\"time\", tm.assert_frame_equal(result, expected)\n\n    def test_multiby(self):\n        # GH13936\n        trades = pd.DataFrame(\n            {\n                \"time\": to_datetime(\n           [\n                [to_datetime(\"20160602\"), 1, \"a\"],\n                [to_datetime(\"20160602\"), 2, \"a\"],\n                [to_datetime(\"20160603\"), 1, \"b\"],\n                [to_datetime(\"20160603\"), 2, \"b\"],\n            ],\n            columns=[\"time\", \"k1\", \"k2\"],\n        ).set_index(\"time\")\n        # different dtype for the index\n        left.index = left.index - pd.Timestamp(0)\n\n        right = pd.DataFrame(\n            [\n                [to_datetime(\"20160502\"), 1, \"a\", 1.0],\n                [to_datetime(\"20160502\"), 2, \"a\", 2.0],\n                [to_datetime(\"20160503\"), 1, \"b\", 3.0],\n                [to_datetime(\"20160503\"), 2, \"b\", 4.0],\n            ],\n            columns=[\"time\", \"k1\", \"k2\", \"value\"],\n        ).set_index(\"time\")\n\n        msg = \"incompatible merge keys\"\n        with pytest.raises(MergeError, match=msg):\n            merge_asof(left, right, left_index=True, right_index=True, by=[\"k1\", \"k2\"])\n\n    def test_multiby_indexed(self):\n        # GH15676\n        left = pd.DataFrame(\n            [\n                [to_datetime(\"20160602\"), 1, \"a\"],\n                [to_datetime(\"20160602\"), 2, \"a\"],\n                [to_datetime(\"20160603\"), 1, \"b\"],\n                [to_datetime(\"20160603\"), 2, \"b\"],\n            ],\n            columns=[\"time\", \"k1\", \"k2\"],\n        ).set_index(\"time\")\n\n        right = pd.DataFrame(\n            [\n                [to_datetime(\"20160502\"), 1, \"a\", 1.0],\n                [to_datetime(\"20160502\"), 2, \"a\", 2.0],\n                [to_datetime(\"20160503\"), 1, \"b\", 3.0],\n                [to_datetime(\"20160503\"), 2, \"b\", 4.0],\n            ],\n            columns=[\"time\", \"k1\", \"k2\", \"value\"],\n        ).set_index(\"time\")\n\n        expected = pd.DataFrame(\n            [\n                [to_datetime(\"20160602\"), 1, \"a\", 1.0],\n                [to_datetime(\"20160602\"), 2, \"a\", 2.0],\n                [to_datetime(\"20160603\"), 1, \"b\", 3.0],\n                [to_datetime(\"20160603\"), 2, \"b\", 4.0],\n            ],\n            columns=[\"time\", \"k1\", \"k2\", \"value\"],\n        ).set_index(\"time\")\n\n        result = merge_asof(\n            left, right, left_index=True, right_index=True, by=[\"k1\", \"k2\"]\n        )\n\n        tm.assert_frame_equal(expected, result)\n\n        with pytest.raises(\n            MergeError, match=\"left_by and right_by must be the same length\"\n        ):\n            merge_asof(\n                left,\n                right,\n                left_index=True,\n                right_index=True,\n                left_by=[\"k1\", \"k2\"],\n                right_by=[\"k1\"],\n            )\n\n    def test_basic2(self, datapath):\n        expected = pd.DataFrame(\n            [\n                [\n                    \"20160525 13:30:00.023\",\n tm.assert_frame_equal(result, expected)\n\n    def test_basic_no_by(self, trades, asof, quotes):\n        f = (\n            lambda x: x[x.ticker == \"MSFT\"]\n            .drop(\"ticker\", axis=1)\n            .reset_index(drop=True)\n        )\n\n        # just use a single ticker\n        expected = f(asof)\n        trades = f(trades)\n        quotes = f(quotes)\n\n        result = merge_asof(trades, quotes, on=\"time\")\n        tm.assert_frame_equal(result, expected)\n\n    def test_valid_join_keys(self, trades, quotes):\n        msg = r\"incompatible merge keys \\[1\\] .* must be the same type\"\n\n        with pytest.raises(MergeError, match=msg):\n            merge_asof(trades, quotes, left_on=\"time\", right_on=\"bid\", by=\"ticker\")\n\n        with pytest.raises(MergeError, match=\"can only asof on a key for left\"):\n            merge_asof(trades, quotes, on=[\"time\", \"ticker\"], by=\"ticker\")\n\n        with pytest.raises(MergeError, match=\"can only asof on a key for left\"):\n            merge_asof(trades, quotes, by=\"ticker\")\n\n    def test_with_duplicates(self, datapath, trades, quotes, asof):\n        q = (\n            pd.concat([quotes, quotes])\n            .sort_values([\"time\", \"ticker\"])\n            .reset_index(drop=True)\n        )\n        result = merge_asof(trades, q, on=\"time\", by=\"ticker\")\n        expected = self.prep_data(asof)\n        tm.assert_frame_equal(result, expected)\n\n    def test_with_duplicates_no_on(self):\n        df1 = pd.DataFrame({\"key\": [1, 1, 3], \"left_val\": [1, 2, 3]})\n        df2 = pd.DataFrame({\"key\": [1, 2, 2], \"right_val\": [1, 2, 3]})\n        result = merge_asof(df1, df2, on=\"key\")\n        expected = pd.DataFrame(\n            {\"key\": [1, 1, 3], \"left_val\": [1, 2, 3], \"right_val\": [1, 1, 3]}\n        )\n        tm.assert_frame_equal(result, expected)\n\n    def test_valid_allow_exact_matches(self, trades, quotes):\n        msg = \"allow_exact_matches must be boolean, passed foo\"\n\n        with pytest.raises(MergeError, match=msg):\n            merge_asof(\n                trades, quotes, on=\"time\", by=\"ticker\", allow_exact_matches=\"foo\"\n            )\n\n    def test_valid_tolerance(self, trades, quotes):\n        # dti\n        merge_asof(trades, quotes, on=\"time\", by=\"ticker\", tolerance=Timedelta(\"1s\"))\n\n        # integer\n        merge_asof(\n            trades.reset_index(),\n            quotes.reset_index(),\n            on=\"index\",\n            by=\"ticker\",\n            tolerance=1,\n        )\n\n        msg = r\"incompatible tolerance .*, must be compat with type .*\"\n\n        # incompat\n        with pytest.raises(MergeError, match=msg):\n            merge_asof(trades, quotes, on=\"time\", by=\"ticker\", tolerance=1)\n\n        # invalid\n        with pytest.raises(MergeError, match=msg):\n            merge_asof(\n                trades.reset_index(),\n                quotes.reset_index(),\n                on=\"index\",\n                by=\"ticker\",\n                tolerance=1.0,\n            )\n\n        msg = \"tolerance must be positive\"\n\n        # invalid negative\n        with pytest.raises(MergeError, match=msg):\n            merge_asof(\n                trades, quotes, on=\"time\", by=\"ticker\", tolerance=-Timedelta(\"1s\")\n            )\n\n        with pytest.raises(MergeError, match=msg):\n            merge_asof(\n                trades.reset_index(),\n                quotes.reset_index(),\n                on=\"index\",\n                by=\"ticker\",\n                tolerance=-1,\n            )\n\n    def test_non_sorted(self, trades, quotes):\n        trades = trades.sort_values(\"time\", ascending=False)\n        quotes = quotes.sort_values(\"time\", ascending=False)\n\n        # we require that we are already sorted on time & quotes\n        assert not trades.time.is_monotonic_increasing\n        assert not quotes.time.is_monotonic_increasing\n        with pytest.raises(ValueError, match=\"left keys must be sorted\"):\n            merge_asof(trades, quotes, on=\"time\", by=\"ticker\")\n\n        trades = trades.sort_values(\"time\")\n        assert trades.time.is_monotonic_increasing\n        assert not quotes.time.is_monotonic_increasing\n        with pytest.raises(ValueError, match=\"right keys must be sorted\"):\n            merge_asof(trades, quotes, on=\"time\", by=\"ticker\")\n\n        quotes = quotes.sort_values(\"time\")\n        assert trades.time.is_monotonic_increasing\n        assert quotes.time.is_monotonic_increasing\n\n        # ok, though has dupes\n        merge_asof(trades, quotes, on=\"time\", by=\"ticker\")\n\n test_tolerance(self, tolerance_ts, trades, quotes, tolerance):\n        result = merge_asof(\n            trades, quotes, on=\"time\", by=\"ticker\", tolerance=tolerance_ts\n        )\n        expected = tolerance\n        tm.assert_frame_equal(result, expected)\n\n    def test_tolerance_forward(self):\n        # GH14887\n\n        left = pd.DataFrame({\"a\": [1, 5, 10], \"left_val\": minimum-price variation\n        df1 = pd.DataFrame(\n            {\n                \"price\": [5.01, 0.0023, 25.13, 340.05, 30.78, 1040.90, 0.0078],\n                \"symbol\": list(\"ABCDEFG\"),\n            },\n            columns=[\"symbol\", \"price\"],\n        )\n\n        df2 = pd.DataFrame(\n            {\"price\": [0.0, 1.0, 100.0], \"mpv\": [0.0001, 0.01, 0.05]},\n            columns=[\"price\", \"mpv\"],\n        )\n\n        df1 = df1.sort_values(\"price\").reset_index(drop=True)\n\n        result = merge_asof(df1, df2, on=\"price\")\n\n        expected = pd.DataFrame(\n            {\n                \"symbol\": list(\"BGACEDF\"),\n                \"price\": [0.0023, 0.0078, 5.01, 25.13, 30.78, 340.05, 1040.90],\n                \"mpv\": [0.0001, 0.0001, 0.01, 0.01, any_real_numpy_dtype):\n        # see gh-13936\n        dtype = np.dtype(any_real_numpy_dtype).type\n\n        df1 = pd.DataFrame(\n            {\"value\": [5, 2, 25, 100, 78, 120, 79], \"symbol\": list(\"ABCDEFG\")},\n            columns=[\"symbol\", \"value\"],\n        )\n        df1.value = dtype(df1.value)\n\n        df2 = pd.DataFrame(\n            {\"value\": [0, 80, 120, 125], \"result\": list(\"xyzw\")},\n            columns=[\"value\", \"result\"],\n        )\n        df2.value = dtype(df2.value)\n\n        df1 = df1.sort_values(\"value\").reset_index(drop=True)\n        result = merge_asof(df1, df2, on=\"value\")\n\n        expected = pd.DataFrame(\n            {\n                \"symbol\": list(\"BACEGDF\"),\n                \"value\": [2, 5, 25, 78, 79, 100, 120],\n                \"result\": list(\"xxxxxyz\"),\n            },\n            columns=[\"symbol\", \"value\", \"result\"],\n        )\n        expected.value = dtype(expected.value)\n\n        tm.assert_frame_equal(result, expected)\n\n    def test_on_specialized_type_by_int(self, any_real_numpy_dtype):\n        # see gh-13936\n        dtype = np.dtype(any_real_numpy_dtype).type\n\n        df1 = pd.DataFrame(\n            {\n                \"value\": [5, 2, 25, 100, 78, 120, 79],\n                \"key\": [1, 2, 3, 2, 3, 1, 2],\n                \"symbol\": list(\"ABCDEFG\"),\n            },\n            columns=[\"symbol\", \"key\", \"value\"],\n        )\n        df1.value = dtype(df1.value)\n\n        df2 = pd.DataFrame(\n            {\"value\": [0, 80, 120, 125], \"key\": [1, 2, 2, 3], \"result\": list(\"xyzw\")},\n            columns=[\"value\", \"key\", \"result\"],\n        )\n        df2.value = dtype(df2.value)\n\n        df1 = df1.sort_values(\"value\").reset_index(drop=True)\n        result = merge_asof(df1, df2, on=\"value\", by=\"key\")\n\n        expected = pd.DataFrame(\n            {\n                \"symbol\": list(\"BACEGDF\"),\n                \"key\": [2, 1, 3, 3, 2, 2, 1],\n                \"value\": [2, 5, 25, 78, 79, 100, 120],\n                \"result\": [np.nan, \"x\", np.nan, np.nan, np.nan, \"y\", \"x\"],\n            },\n            columns=[\"symbol\", \"key\", \"value\", \"result\"],\n        )\n        expected.value = dtype(expected.value)\n\n        tm.assert_frame_equal(result, expected)\n\n    def test_on_float_by_int(self):\n        # type specialize both \"by\" and \"on\" parameters\n        df1 = pd.DataFrame(\n            {\n                \"symbol\": list(\"AAABBBCCC\"),\n                \"exch\": [1, 2, 3, 1, 2, 3, 1, 2, 3],\n                \"price\": [\n                    3.26,\n                    3.2599,\n                    3.2598,\n                    12.58,\n                    12.59,\n                    12.5,\n                    378.15,\n                    378.2,\n                    378.25,\n                ],\n            },\n            columns=[\"symbol\", \"exch\", \"price\"],\n        )\n\n        df2 = pd.DataFrame(\n            {\n                \"exch\": [1, 1, 1, 2, 2, 2, 3, 3, 3],\n                \"price\": [0.0, 1.0, 100.0, 0.0, 5.0, 100.0, 0.0, 5.0, 1000.0],\n                \"mpv\": [0.0001, 0.01, 0.05, 0.0001, 0.01, 0.1, 0.0001, 0.25, 1.0],\n            },\n            columns=[\"exch\", \"price\", \"mpv\"],\n        )\n\n        df1 = df1.sort_values(\"price\").reset_index(drop=True)\n        df2 = df2.sort_values(\"price\").reset_index(drop=True)\n\n        result = merge_asof(df1, df2, on=\"price\", by=\"exch\")\n\n        expected = pd.DataFrame(\n            {\n                \"symbol\": list(\"AAABBBCCC\"),\n                \"exch\": [3, 2, 1, 3, 1, 2, 1, 2, 3],\n                \"price\": [\n                    3.2598,\n                    3.2599,\n                    3.26,\n                    12.5,\n                    12.58,\n                    12.59,\n                    378.15,\n                    378.2,\n                    378.25,\n                ],\n                \"mpv\": [0.0001, 0.0001, 0.01, 0.25, 0.01, 0.01, 0.05, 0.1, 0.25],\n            },\n            columns=[\"symbol\", \"exch\", \"price\", \"mpv\"],\n        )\n\n        tm.assert_frame_equal(result, expected)\n\n    def test_merge_datatype_error_raises(self, using_infer_string):\n        if using_infer_string:\n            msg = \"incompatible merge keys\"\n        else:\n            msg = r\"Incompatible merge dtype, .*, both sides must have numeric dtype\"\n\n        left = pd.DataFrame({\"left_val\": [1, 5, 10], \"a\": [\"a\", \"b\", \"c\"]})\n        right = pd.DataFrame({\"right_val\": [1, 2, 3, 6, 7], \"a\": [1, 2, 3, 6, 7]})\n\n        with pytest.raises(MergeError, match=msg):\n            merge_asof(left, right, on=\"a\")\n\n    def test_merge_datatype_categorical_error_raises(self):\n        msg = (\n            r\"incompatible merge keys \\[0\\] .* both sides category, \"\n            \"but not equal ones\"\n        )\n\n        left = pd.DataFrame(\n            {\"left_val\": [1, 5, 10], \"a\": pd.Categorical([\"a\", \"b\", \"c\"])}\n        )\n        right = pd.DataFrame(\n            {\n                \"right_val\": [1, 2, 3, 6, 7],\n                \"a\": pd.Categorical([\"a\", \"X\", \"c\", \"X\", \"b\"]),\n            }\n        )\n\n        with pytest.raises(MergeError, match=msg):\n            merge_asof(left, right, on=\"a\")\n\n    def test_merge_groupby_multiple_column_with_categorical_column(self):\n        # GH 16454\n        df = pd.DataFrame({\"x\": [0], \"y\": [0], \"z\": pd.Categorical([0])})\n        result = merge_asof(df, df, on=\"x\", by=[\"y\", \"z\"])\n        expected = pd.DataFrame({\"x\": [0], \"y\": [0], \"z\": pd.Categorical([0])})\n        tm.assert_frame_equal(result, expected)\n\n    @pytest.mark.parametrize(\n        \"func\", [lambda x: x, lambda x: to_datetime(x)], ids=[\"numeric\", \"datetime\"]\n    )\n    @pytest.mark.parametrize(\"side\", [\"left\", \"right\"])\n    def test_merge_on_nans(self, func, side):\n        # GH 23189\n        msg = f\"Merge keys contain null values on {side} side\"\n        nulls = func([1.0, 5.0, np.nan])\n        non_nulls = func([1.0, 5.0, 10.0])\n        df_null = pd.DataFrame({\"a\": nulls, \"left_val\": [\"a\", \"b\", \"c\"]})\n        df = pd.DataFrame({\"a\": non_nulls, \"right_val\": [1, 6, 11]})\n\n        with pytest.raises(ValueError, match=msg):\n            if side == \"left\":\n                merge_asof(df_null, df, on=\"a\")\n            else:\n                merge_asof(df, df_null, on=\"a\")\n\n    def test_by_nullable(self, any_numeric_ea_dtype, using_infer_string):\n        # Note: this test passes if instead of using pd.array we use\n        #  np.array([np.nan, 1]).  Other than that, I (@jbrockmendel)\n        #  have NO IDEA what the expected behavior is.\n        # TODO(GH#32306): may be relevant to the expected behavior here.\n\n        arr = pd.array([pd.NA, 0, 1], dtype=any_numeric_ea_dtype)\n        if arr.dtype.kind in [\"i\", \"u\"]:\n            max_val = np.iinfo(arr.dtype.numpy_dtype).max\n        else:\n            max_val = np.finfo(arr.dtype.numpy_dtype).max\n        # set value s.t. GH 27642\n        if unit == \"s\":\n            pytest.skip(\n                \"This test is invalid with unit='s' because that would \"\n                \"round left['time']\"\n            )\n\n        left = pd.DataFrame(\n            list(zip([0, 5, 10, 15, 20, 25], [0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5])),\n            columns=[\"time\", \"left\"],\n        )\n\n        left[\"time\"] = pd.to_timedelta(left[\"time\"], \"ms\").astype(f\"m8[{unit}]\")\n\n        right = pd.DataFrame(\n            list(zip([0, 3, 9, 12, 15, 18], [0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5])),\n            columns=[\"time\", \"right\"],\n        )\n\n        right[\"time\"] = pd.to_timedelta(right[\"time\"], \"ms\").astype(f\"m8[{unit}]\")\n\n        expected = pd.DataFrame(\n            list(\n                zip(\n                    [0, 5, 10, 15, 20, 25],\n                    [0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5],\n                    [0, np.nan, 2, 4, np.nan, np.nan],\n                )\n            ),\n            columns=[\"time\", \"left\", \"right\"],\n        )\n\n        expected[\"time\"] = pd.to_timedelta(expected[\"time\"], \"ms\").astype(f\"m8[{unit}]\")\n\n        result = merge_asof(\n            left, right, on=\"time\", tolerance=Timedelta(\"1ms\"), direction=\"nearest\"\n        )\n\n        tm.assert_frame_equal(result, expected)\n\n    def test_int_type_tolerance(self, any_int_dtype):\n        # The indicator column shows which table each row originated from ('both', 'left_only', or 'right_only').\n\n## Changes Made\n\n### 1. Modified `/workspace/pandas/core/reshape/merge.py`\n\n#### 1.1 Updated `merge_asof()` function signature (line 440-456)\n- **Added parameter**: `indicator: str | bool = False`\n- **Purpose**: Allows users to request an indicator column showing merge source\n\n#### 1.2 Updated `merge_asof()` docstring (line 514-517)\n- **Added documentation**: Describes the indicator parameter, its values, and usage with custom names\n\n#### 1.3 Updated `merge_asof()` function call to `_AsOfMerge` (line 712)\n- **Change**: Passes `indicator=indicator` to the `_AsOfMerge` constructor\n\n#### 1.4 Updated `_OrderedMerge.__init__()` signature (line 1903-1915)\n- **Added parameter**: `indicator: str | bool = False`\n- **Change**: Passes `indicator=indicator` to parent `_MergeOperation.__init__()` (line 1930)\n\n#### 1.5 Updated `_AsOfMerge.__init__()` signature (line 1970-1987)\n- **Added parameter**: `indicator: str | bool = False`\n- **Change**: Passes `indicator=indicator` to parent `_OrderedMerge.__init__()` (line 2020)\n\n#### 1.6 Added `_AsOfMerge.get_result()` method (lines 2309-2327)\n- **Implementation**: Overrides parent's `get_result()` to handle indicator processing\n- **Logic**:\n  1. If indicator is set, calls `_indicator_pre_merge()` to add temporary indicator columns\n  2. Performs the join operation via `_get_join_info()`\n  3. Concatenates results\n  4. If indicator is set, calls `_indicator_post_merge()` to create final indicator column\n  5. Adds join keys and restores index levels\n  6. Returns finalized result\n- **Inherited methods used**:\n  - `_indicator_pre_merge()`: Adds _left_indicator and _right_indicator columns\n  - `_indicator_post_merge()`: Creates final indicator column with categorical values\n  - `_reindex_and_concat()`: Handles reindexing and concatenation\n  - `_maybe_add_join_keys()`: Manages join key columns\n  - `_maybe_restore_index_levels()`: Restores index levels if needed\n\n### 2. Modified `/workspace/pandas/tests/reshape/merge/test_merge_asof.py`\n\n#### 2.1 Added test: `test_merge_asof_indicator()`\n- Tests basic indicator functionality with `indicator=True`\n- Verifies _merge column exists and has categorical dtype\n- Checks that matched rows are marked as \"both\"\n\n#### 2.2 Added test: `test_merge_asof_indicator_with_custom_name()`\n- Tests indicator with custom column name: `indicator=\"custom_merge\"`\n- Verifies custom column exists with categorical dtype\n\n#### 2.3 Added test: `test_merge_asof_indicator_no_matches()`\n- Tests indicator when some left rows don't have matches in right\n- Verifies \"left_only\" values for unmatched rows\n- Verifies \"both\" values for matched rows\n\n#### 2.4 Added test: `test_merge_asof_indicator_false()`\n- Tests default behavior: `indicator=False`\n- Verifies _merge column is NOT created when indicator=False\n\n#### 2.5 Added test: `test_merge_asof_indicator_with_by()`\n- Tests indicator works with the `by` parameter\n- Verifies indicator column is created even with grouping\n\n## Design Decisions\n\n### 1. Inheritance Pattern\n- Leveraged existing `_indicator_pre_merge()` and `_indicator_post_merge()` methods from `_MergeOperation`\n- No code duplication; reuses proven indicator handling logic\n\n### 2. Method Override\n- Overrode `get_result()` in `_AsOfMerge` rather than modifying `_OrderedMerge.get_result()`\n- This allows _AsOfMerge to use indicator-aware logic while keeping _OrderedMerge unchanged\n- Maintains separation of concerns and reduces impact on existing code\n\n### 3. Parameter Passing\n- Updated both `_OrderedMerge.__init__()` and `_AsOfMerge.__init__()` to accept indicator\n- This ensures indicator can be used with merge_ordered in the future if needed\n\n### 4. Validation\n- Leverages `_indicator_name` property from `_MergeOperation` for validation\n- Automatically validates that indicator is bool or str\n- Raises ValueError for invalid types\n\n## Behavior\n\n### merge_asof with indicator=True\n```python\nleft = pd.DataFrame({\"a\": [1, 5, 10], \"left_val\": [\"a\", \"b\", \"c\"]})\nright = pd.DataFrame({\"a\": [1, 2, 3, 6, 7], \"right_val\": [1, 2, 3, 6, 7]})\nresult = pd.merge_asof(left, right, on=\"a\", indicator=True)\n# Result includes '_merge' column with values:\n# 0: 'both'      (matched with a=1)\n# 1: 'both'      (matched with a=3)\n# 2: 'both'      (matched with a=7)\n```\n\n### merge_asof with indicator=\"custom_col\"\n```python\nresult = pd.merge_asof(left, right, on=\"a\", indicator=\"custom_col\")\n# Result includes 'custom_col' instead of '_merge'\n```\n\n### merge_asof with indicator=False (default)\n```python\nresult = pd.merge_asof(left, right, on=\"a\")\n# No indicator column added\n```\n\n## Compatibility\n\n- **Backwards compatible**: Default value is `indicator=False`, maintaining existing behavior\n- **Consistent with merge()**: Uses same indicator semantics and values as `pd.merge()`\n- **Works with all merge_asof parameters**: Compatible with `by`, `tolerance`, `direction`, etc.\n\n## Testing\n\nAll test cases cover:\n- Basic indicator functionality\n- Custom column names\n- Handling of unmatched rows\n- Default behavior (no indicator)\n- Interaction with `by` parameter\n\n## Files Modified\n\n1. `/workspace/pandas/core/reshape/merge.py` - Tests custom column naming with `indicator=\"custom_merge\"`\n   - Verifies custom column name is used instead of `_merge`\n\n3. **`test_merge_asof_indicator_no_matches()`** (lines 762-778)\n   - Tests handling of unmatched rows\n   - Left row with no match in right gets \"left_only\"\n   - Matched rows get \"both\"\n\n4. **`test_merge_asof_indicator_false()`** (lines 781-790)\n   - Tests default behavior (no indicator column added)\n   - Verifies `_merge` column is NOT created when `indicator=False`\n\n5. **`test_merge_asof_indicator_with_by()`** (lines 793-820)\n   - Tests indicator works correctly with the `by` parameter\n   - Verifies indicator column is created alongside grouped merge\n\n## Implementation Details\n\n### Design Pattern\nThe implementation follows the existing pattern used in `pd.merge()`:\n- Reuses the `_indicator_pre_merge()` and `_indicator_post_merge()` methods from `_MergeOperation`\n- No code duplication; leverages proven indicator handling logic\n- Overrides `get_result()` in `_AsOfMerge` to integrate indicator processing\n\n### Method Override Strategy\nRather than modifying `_OrderedMerge.get_result()` (which is also used by `merge_ordered()`), the implementation adds a new `get_result()` method to `_AsOfMerge` that:\n1. Handles indicator pre-processing before the join\n2. Performs the asof merge\n3. Handles indicator post-processing after the join\n4. Properly manages all join keys and index levels\n\n### Validation\nValidation is automatically provided by the inherited `_indicator_name` property in `_MergeOperation`:\n- Raises `ValueError` if indicator is not a bool or str\n- Raises `ValueError` if the indicator column name conflicts with existing columns\n\n## Behavior Examples\n\n### Basic Usage\n```python\nleft = pd.DataFrame({\"a\": [1, 5, 10], \"left_val\": [\"a\", \"b\", \"c\"]})\nright = pd.DataFrame({\"a\": [1, 2, 3, 6, 7], \"right_val\": [1, 2, 3, 6, 7]})\n\nresult = pd.merge_asof(left, right, on=\"a\", indicator=True)\n# Result will include '_merge' column with values 'both' for all rows\n```\n\n### Custom Column Name\n```python\nresult = pd.merge_asof(left, right, on=\"a\", indicator=\"merge_status\")\n# Result will include 'merge_status' column instead of '_merge'\n```\n\n### Unmatched Rows\n```python\nleft = pd.DataFrame({\"a\": [0.5, 5, 10], \"left_val\": [\"a\", \"b\", \"c\"]})\nright = pd.DataFrame({\"a\": [1, 2, 3], \"right_val\": [1, 2, 3]})\n\nresult = pd.merge_asof(left, right, on=\"a\", indicator=True)\n# Row a=0.5 has \"left_only\" (no match in right)\n# Rows a=5, a=10 have \"both\" (matched with a=3)\n```\n\n## Testing\n\nAll tests validate:\n- ✓ Basic indicator functionality with default column name '_merge'\n- ✓ Custom column naming for the indicator\n- ✓ Correct values for matched and unmatched rows\n- ✓ Default behavior when indicator=False\n- ✓ Compatibility with other merge_asof parameters (by, etc.)\n\n## Backwards Compatibility\n\n✓ **Fully backwards compatible**\n- Default value is `indicator=False`\n- Existing code without indicator parameter works unchanged\n- No breaking changes to API or behavior\n\n## Consistency with pd.merge()\n\n✓ **Follows existing pattern**\n- Calls `_indicator_pre_merge()` before join to add temporary indicator columns\n  - Performs the asof merge\n  - Calls `_indicator_post_merge()` after join to create the final indicator column\n  - Properly integrates with existing join key and index level handling\n\n### 2. `/workspace/pandas/tests/reshape/merge/test_merge_asof.py`\n\nAdded five comprehensive test functions:\n\n1. **`test_merge_asof_indicator()`** (lines 729-743)\n   - Tests basic indicator functionality with `indicator=True`\n   - Verifies `_merge` column exists and contains correct values\n   - Verifies categorical dtype\n\n2. **`test_merge_asof_indicator_with_custom_name()`** (lines 746-759)\n   - Tests custom column naming with `indicator=\"custom_merge\"`\n   - Verifies custom column name is used instead of `_merge`\n\n3. **`test_merge_asof_indicator_no_matches()`** (lines 762-778)\n   - Tests handling of unmatched rows\n   - Left row with no match in right gets \"left_only\"\n   - Matched rows get \"both\"\n\n4. **`test_merge_asof_indicator_false()`** (lines 781-790)\n   - Tests default behavior (no indicator column added)\n   - Verifies `_merge` column is NOT created when `indicator=False`\n\n5. **`test_merge_asof_indicator_with_by()`** (lines 793-820)\n   - Tests indicator works correctly with the `by` parameter\n   - Verifies indicator column is created alongside grouped merge\n\n## Implementation Details\n\n### Design Pattern\nThe implementation follows the existing pattern used in `pd.merge()`:\n- Reuses the `_indicator_pre_merge()` and `_indicator_post_merge()` methods from `_MergeOperation`\n- No code duplication; leverages proven indicator handling logic\n- Overrides `get_result()` in `_AsOfMerge` to integrate indicator processing\n\n### Method Override Strategy\nRather than modifying `_OrderedMerge.get_result()` (which is also used by `merge_ordered()`), the implementation adds a new `get_result()` method to `_AsOfMerge` that:\n1. Handles indicator pre-processing before the join\n2. Performs the asof merge\n3. Handles indicator post-processing after the join\n4. Properly manages all join keys and index levels\n\n### Validation\nValidation is automatically provided by the inherited `_indicator_name` property in `_MergeOperation`:\n- Raises `ValueError` if indicator is not a bool or str\n- Raises `ValueError` if the indicator column name conflicts with existing columns\n\n## Behavior Examples\n\n### Basic Usage\n```python\nleft = pd.DataFrame({\"a\": [1, 5, 10], \"left_val\": [\"a\", \"b\", \"c\"]})\nright = pd.DataFrame({\"a\": [1, 2, 3, 6, 7], \"right_val\": [1, 2, 3, 6, 7]})\n\nresult = pd.merge_asof(left, right, on=\"a\", indicator=True)\n# Result will include '_merge' column with values 'both' for all rows\n```\n\n### Custom Column Name\n```python\nresult = pd.merge_asof(left, right, on=\"a\", indicator=\"merge_status\")\n# Result will include 'merge_status' column instead of '_merge'\n```\n\n### Unmatched Rows\n```python\nleft = pd.DataFrame({\"a\": [0.5, 5, 10], \"left_val\": [\"a\", \"b\", \"c\"]})\nright = pd.DataFrame({\"a\": [1, 2, 3], \"right_val\": [1, 2, 3]})\n\nresult = pd.merge_asof(left, right, on=\"a\", indicator=True)\n# Row a=0.5 has \"left_only\" (no match in right)\n# Rows a=5, a=10 have \"both\" (matched with a=3)\n```\n\n## Testing\n\nAll tests validate:\n- ✓ Basic indicator functionality with default column name '_merge'\n- ✓ Custom column naming for the indicator\n- ✓ Correct values for matched and unmatched rows\n- ✓ Default behavior when indicator=False\n- ✓ Compatibility with other merge_asof parameters (by, etc.)\n\n## Backwards Compatibility\n\n✓ **Fully backwards compatible**\n- Default
